content.claim: National class-size and per-pupil funding formulas create structural fiscal pressure on small village schools independent of governance structure, with minimum class sizes (14-27 students for primary grades) and funding reductions when enrollment falls below legal maximum thresholds.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Precise contemporary parameters (post-2017) were not independently re-verified for 2026
Mechanism is established but exact current funding formula coefficients require update verification
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
